winner.title: HumanUniqueness proofs go stale instantly — no root history buffer
winner.description: IdentityRegistry maintains a 30-root history buffer for agentTree but has NO equivalent for humanTree. The contract checks `humanTree._root()` (current root only) against the humanMerkleRoot public output. Any new enrollment between proof generation and on-chain verification invalidates the proof. This is a liveness denial-of-service: a single enrollment transaction front-runs every in-flight human proof. Add a `humanRootHistory[30]` ring buffer identical to `agentRootHistory`, update it in `enrollHuman()`, and check `humanMerkleRoot` against the buffer in `verifyHandshake()`.

outline.title: HumanUniqueness proofs go stale instantly — no root history buffer

artifacts.description: Add `humanRootHistory[30]` ring buffer storage, a `humanRootHistoryIndex` pointer, push current root into the buffer inside `enrollHuman()`, and replace the `humanTree._root()` point-in-time check inside `verifyHandshake()` with a loop over the 30-slot buffer.

Hardhat tests covering: (1) proof generated before enrollment still verifies within window, (2) proof older than 30 enrollments is correctly rejected, (3) ring wrap-around at slot 30, (4) simultaneous human + agent enrollment does not corrupt either ring, (5) front-run simulation — second enrollment between proof generation and verifyHandshake call still succeeds.
Document the 30-root history window for humanTree root acceptance, matching the existing agentTree window language. Note that the window is enrollment-count-based, not time-based.
Update the liveness / DoS threat row for HumanUniqueness to mark it mitigated via history buffer; cite the enrollment-count window and its trade-offs.

steps: Read contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol to locate agentRootHistory ring buffer pattern (storage layout, push logic in enroll, loop check in verify).

Mirror the pattern: add `bytes32[30] private humanRootHistory` and `uint256 private humanRootHistoryIndex` storage variables immediately after the agentTree equivalents to keep storage slot ordering stable.
In `enrollHuman()`: after `humanTree.insert(leaf)` succeeds, store the new root — `humanRootHistory[humanRootHistoryIndex % 30] = humanTree._root(); humanRootHistoryIndex++;`
Seed the buffer at deploy time (or first enrollment): if `humanRootHistoryIndex == 0` pre-insert, push the initial empty root so slot 0 is valid before any proof can be generated.

In `verifyHandshake()`: replace the single equality check `require(proof.humanMerkleRoot == humanTree._root())` with a loop across all 30 slots, reverting only if none match — use a local `bool found` flag to avoid repeated SLOADs.
Gas-profile the 30-iteration loop against the baseline single-root check; add a NatSpec note with the worst-case gas delta so integrators can size their gas limits.
Write the Hardhat test file covering all five scenarios listed in the test artifact, including the front-run simulation using `evm_snapshot` / `evm_revert` to bracket the enrollment transaction.
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md to document the acceptance window semantics (enrollment-count-based, not time-based).
Update docs/owasp-agentic-mapping.md liveness/DoS row.
Run `npm run test:contracts` and confirm all existing tests pass plus new tests pass.
Commit with DCO sign-off: `git commit -s -m 'fix: add humanRootHistory[30] ring buffer to IdentityRegistry'`
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — agentRootHistory pattern must be read before mirroring
IncrementalMerkleTree library — confirm _root() returns the post-insert root synchronously
Hardhat test suite — existing verifyHandshake integration tests must remain green after the loop replacement

winner.title: Add human tree root history buffer matching agent tree's 30-root buffer
winner.description: IdentityRegistry.sol maintains a 30-root history buffer for agentTree but humanTree has none — any new enrollment instantly invalidates all in-flight human proofs. This is a protocol-level inconsistency that will cause silent failures in production. Add the same ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 circular buffer for humanTree roots, a mapping(uint256 => bool) knownHumanRoots, and update verifyHandshake to check knownHumanRoots[humanMerkleRoot] instead of comparing against the single current root. Deliverable: updated IdentityRegistry.sol, Hardhat test proving a proof generated before a new enrollment still verifies, and spec language in the IETF draft §4.2.

outline.title: Add human tree root history buffer matching agent tree's 30-root buffer

artifacts.description: Add ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 constant, humanRootHistory circular buffer array, humanRootIndex uint256 counter, knownHumanRoots mapping(uint256=>bool), and _addHumanRoot() internal helper. Update enroll() to call _addHumanRoot() instead of direct assignment. Update verifyHandshake() to check knownHumanRoots[humanMerkleRoot] instead of humanTree.root == humanMerkleRoot.

Hardhat test suite: (1) enroll N users, prove that a proof generated at root R_k still verifies after up to 29 subsequent enrollments; (2) prove that a proof generated at root R_k fails after 30 subsequent enrollments (buffer eviction); (3) prove the current root is always in knownHumanRoots; (4) fuzz test with ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE boundary (enroll exactly 30 and 31 times).

Update §4.2 to specify that both humanTree and agentTree maintain a ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 circular root history buffer. Verifiers MUST accept any root in the history window. Define the eviction policy (oldest root evicted first). Add MUST-level requirement that implementations maintain parity between human and agent root history sizes.
Document the breaking change: verifyHandshake now accepts historical human roots. Note that any existing deployment must be redeployed as the storage layout changes.

steps: Read contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol to understand current agentTree root history implementation and mirror the exact pattern for humanTree.
Add `uint256 constant ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE = 30;` alongside the existing agent constant (or confirm it is already shared).
Add storage: `uint256[30] public humanRootHistory; uint256 public humanRootIndex; mapping(uint256 => bool) public knownHumanRoots;`

Implement `_addHumanRoot(uint256 root) internal`: evict the slot at humanRootIndex from knownHumanRoots, write new root into humanRootHistory[humanRootIndex % ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE], set knownHumanRoots[root] = true, increment humanRootIndex.
Update `enroll()` (or equivalent function that updates humanTree.root) to call `_addHumanRoot(humanTree.root)` after the Merkle insertion.
Update `verifyHandshake()` to replace `require(humanTree.root == humanMerkleRoot)` with `require(knownHumanRoots[humanMerkleRoot], 'unknown human root')`.

Write contracts/test/IdentityRegistry.humanRootHistory.test.ts: enroll user A, capture root R0, enroll 29 more users, verify proof with root R0 still passes, enroll one more user (evicts R0), verify proof with R0 now reverts.
Run `npm run test:contracts` and confirm all existing tests still pass alongside the new history tests.
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md §4.2 with MUST-level parity requirement and eviction policy definition.
Commit with DCO sign-off: `git commit -s -m 'fix(contracts): add 30-root history buffer for humanTree matching agentTree'`
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — must read agentTree root history implementation before writing humanTree equivalent
IncrementalMerkleTree library or equivalent used by humanTree — enroll() must expose the new root after insertion
contracts/test/ existing fixtures — new tests should reuse enrollment helpers to avoid duplication
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md §4.2 — must locate exact section before editing to avoid merge conflicts with other spec PRs

winner.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors (JSON) for all three circuits
winner.description: No implementation can claim Bolyra conformance without shared test vectors. Create a spec/test-vectors/ directory with JSON files covering: valid handshake (human+agent), expired agent credential, revoked human identity, stale Merkle root, scope subset violation, cumulative bit encoding violation, delegation chain at depth 1/2/3, and nonce replay. Each vector includes: circuit name, witness inputs, expected public outputs (or expected rejection reason). This is the minimum bar for a second implementer to verify compatibility. Deliverable: 30+ vectors across the three circuits, plus a lightweight Node runner script.

outline.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors (JSON) for all three circuits

artifacts.description: Schema documentation for test vector JSON format, runner invocation, and how to add new vectors
JSON Schema (draft-07) defining the canonical structure for every test vector file
Vectors for HumanUniqueness circuit: valid enrollment, stale Merkle root, nullifier replay (nonce replay), revoked identity, depth boundary
Vectors for AgentPolicy circuit (Groth16 + PLONK): valid credential, expired credential, cumulative-bit encoding violation, scope subset violation, modelHash mismatch
Vectors for Delegation circuit: valid chain depth 1/2/3, scope narrowing valid, scope expansion attempt (must reject), max-depth exceeded, cumulative-bit inheritance violation
End-to-end handshake vectors combining human + agent proofs: valid handshake, nonce mismatch, human proof from different session, agent expired mid-handshake
Lightweight Node.js runner: loads each JSON file, generates witnesses via snarkjs, asserts public outputs match expected or catches expected rejection reasons; exits non-zero on any mismatch
CI pre-check: validates every .json file in spec/test-vectors/ against schema.json before running the witness runner

steps: Audit existing circuit test files (circuits/test/) to extract canonical witness input shapes for HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, and Delegation

Define JSON Schema in spec/test-vectors/schema.json covering fields: id, circuit, provingSystem (groth16|plonk|witness-only), inputs (object), expectedPublicOutputs (object|null), expectedRejection (string|null), tags (array)

Author human-uniqueness.json: 8 vectors — valid enrollment, stale Merkle root (+1 epoch), nullifier replay with same nonce, identity revoked (root absent from on-chain registry stub), depth 19 boundary (valid), depth 21 (invalid), missing commitment leaf, zero secret

Author agent-policy.json: 10 vectors — valid Groth16, valid PLONK, expired timestamp, cumulative-bit violation (bit 3 set without bit 2), scope field exceeding declared max, modelHash all-zeros, operatorPrivKey mismatch, permissions=0 edge case, permissions=0xFF valid, expiryTimestamp=0

Author delegation.json: 10 vectors — chain depth 1 valid, depth 2 valid, depth 3 valid, depth 4 rejection (exceeds max), parent scope ⊃ child scope (valid narrowing), parent scope ⊂ child scope (invalid expansion must reject), cumulative-bit propagation valid, bit dropped at delegation (valid), bit added at delegation (must reject), null parent credential

Author handshake-integration.json: 6 vectors — valid full handshake, nonce mismatch between human and agent proof, human proof replayed in second session, agent credential expired before handshake completion, delegation chain injected into handshake, scope mismatch between human allowlist and agent claim

Implement spec/test-vectors/runner.js: iterate all JSON files, for witness-only vectors use snarkjs.wtns.calculate(), for full proof vectors optionally run groth16/plonk prove+verify when FULL_PROOF=1 env is set; print PASS/FAIL per vector with vector id
Implement spec/test-vectors/validate-schema.js: use ajv to validate each vector file against schema.json; fail fast with clear error on schema violations
Add npm scripts to root package.json: 'test:vectors' (validate-schema + runner with witness-only), 'test:vectors:full' (sets FULL_PROOF=1)
Wire 'test:vectors' into the CI matrix alongside test:circuits:fast so vectors run on every PR
Write spec/test-vectors/README.md: schema field reference, how to generate fixture inputs using the TS SDK, how to add a new vector, and conformance claim requirements (must pass all 30+ vectors)
dependencies: snarkjs ^0.7.x (already a direct dep — witness generation and optional full prove/verify)
ajv ^8 (JSON Schema validation for validate-schema.js)
circuits/build/ artifacts must exist (.wasm files for witness generation) — run npm run compile:circuits first
circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, Delegation.circom — input signal names must be stable before authoring vectors
sdk/src/ — TS SDK used to derive canonical fixture values (commitments, nullifiers, modelHash) for vector inputs

winner.title: Delegation circuit does not verify delegator is enrolled in agentTree
winner.description: The Delegation circuit takes `delegatorCredCommitment` as a private input and uses it to recompute the previousScopeCommitment check, but it never proves that `delegatorCredCommitment` is a leaf in the agentTree Merkle tree. The CIP-1 fix added a Merkle inclusion proof for the *delegatee*, but the *delegator* side relies entirely on chain-linking (previousScopeCommitment matching). An attacker who knows a valid previousScopeCommitment (emitted publicly in events) can forge a delegatorCredCommitment that hashes to the right scopeCommitment without being an enrolled agent. Add a delegator Merkle inclusion proof (reusing the same MAX_DEPTH parameter), or alternatively verify the delegator's AgentPolicy proof on-chain before accepting the delegation and pass the verified agentMerkleRoot as a public input to the delegation circuit.

outline.title: Delegation circuit does not verify delegator is enrolled in agentTree

artifacts.description: Add delegator Merkle inclusion proof inputs (delegatorPathElements, delegatorPathIndices, delegatorTreeDepth) and a MerkleProof component that proves delegatorCredCommitment is a leaf in agentMerkleRoot. Wire agentMerkleRoot as a public input shared with the delegatee proof.
Reuse or extract the existing Merkle inclusion component so both delegatee and delegator paths share the same MAX_DEPTH-parameterized template.

Regenerate from updated vkey.json after re-running trusted setup. Update calldata ABI to include the delegator Merkle root public signal. Add on-chain check that the agentMerkleRoot passed in matches the registry's current root.
Expose getCurrentAgentMerkleRoot() view function so DelegationVerifier can read and validate the root at delegation-acceptance time.
Extend buildDelegationInputs() to accept delegatorMerklePath and delegatorPathIndices, query the AgentRegistry for the current agentMerkleRoot, and pass both into the circuit input object.
Mirror TypeScript changes: update DelegationInputs dataclass and subprocess bridge call to include delegator path fields.

Document the new public input agentMerkleRoot in the delegation proof envelope section. Update the security considerations to note that delegator enrollment is now circuit-enforced, not chain-link-only.

Add negative test: forge a delegatorCredCommitment with valid scopeCommitment hash but no valid Merkle path — witness generation must fail. Add positive test with correct delegator path at varying depths.
Integration test: submit delegation with stale agentMerkleRoot (root rotated after proof generated) — on-chain check must revert. Submit with current root — must accept.
Add entry under 'Broken Object Level Authorization' noting that prior to this fix, a public scopeCommitment event could be replayed by an un-enrolled delegator, and describe the mitigation.

steps: Audit current Delegation.circom to map all existing signals; confirm delegatorCredCommitment is only used in the previousScopeCommitment recompute and is not Merkle-proven.
Extract or confirm MerkleProof.circom template is parameterized by MAX_DEPTH and returns a single root output; make it importable by both Delegation and AgentPolicy.
Add private inputs to Delegation.circom: delegatorPathElements[MAX_DEPTH], delegatorPathIndices[MAX_DEPTH]. Add public input: agentMerkleRoot.
Instantiate a second MerkleProof component in Delegation.circom for the delegator leaf; constrain its computed root === agentMerkleRoot.
Re-run powers-of-tau phase 2 for Delegation using pot16.ptau; regenerate Delegation_groth16.zkey and Delegation_plonk.zkey; export new vkey.json files.
Regenerate DelegationVerifier.sol from the new vkey.json using snarkjs exportSolidityVerifier.
Add getCurrentAgentMerkleRoot() to AgentRegistry.sol; update DelegationVerifier to call it and revert if submitted agentMerkleRoot does not match.
Update sdk/src/delegation.ts buildDelegationInputs() to require delegatorMerklePath parameter and fetch current root from AgentRegistry.
Update sdk-python delegation bridge to pass through the new fields.
Write circuit negative tests (forged delegator path must fail witness gen) and positive tests at depth 1, 8, and MAX_DEPTH.
Write contract integration tests for stale-root rejection and current-root acceptance.
Update spec and OWASP mapping docs.
Run npm run compile:circuits, npm run test:circuits:fast, npm run test:circuits:slow, npm run test:contracts to gate the change.
Regenerate landing/verify.sh expected proof-envelope shape if it checks public signal count.
DCO-sign all commits; update SECURITY.md to close the delegator-unbound advisory.
dependencies: circuits/src/MerkleProof.circom (MAX_DEPTH parameter must be shared)
circuits/build/pot16.ptau (existing universal SRS — no new ceremony needed)
contracts/src/AgentRegistry.sol (must expose agentMerkleRoot view)
snarkjs >= 0.7.6 (for re-keying and Solidity export)
CIP-1 delegatee Merkle inclusion fix (must be merged first to avoid conflict on Delegation.circom)

winner.title: scopeCommitment is brute-forceable — missing blinding salt
winner.description: In AgentPolicy, scopeCommitment = Poseidon2(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment). The permissionBitmask is only 8 meaningful bits (256 values). An observer who knows the credentialCommitment (it's the Merkle leaf, publicly computable from enrollment events) can hash all 256 candidates and recover the exact permission set, breaking the privacy model. Add a private `blindingSalt` input and compute scopeCommitment = Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt). Propagate the same change to Delegation.circom's scope commitment computation and regenerate verifier contracts.

outline.title: scopeCommitment is brute-forceable — missing blinding salt

artifacts.description: Add private signal `blindingSalt`, change scopeCommitment from Poseidon2(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment) to Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt)
Add private signal `blindingSalt`, update scope commitment computation to Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt) to match AgentPolicy
Regenerate Solidity verifier from updated AgentPolicy.zkey after re-running trusted setup
Regenerate Solidity verifier from updated Delegation.zkey after re-running trusted setup
Add blindingSalt generation (32-byte random) in createAgentCredential; include in AgentPolicy proveHandshake input signals; expose blindingSalt in AgentCredential type so callers can persist it
Extend AgentCredential interface with blindingSalt: bigint field
Add blinding_salt: int field to AgentCredential dataclass; update subprocess bridge to pass it through to JS proving
Document blindingSalt as mandatory private input in Section on scope commitment; update threat model to note brute-force defense
Add formal property: scope commitment is computationally hiding — given (scopeCommitment, credentialCommitment), permissionBitmask is indistinguishable without blindingSalt

Add witness-generation tests: (1) same permissions + same credentialCommitment + different blindingSalt → different scopeCommitment; (2) brute-force over 256 bitmask values cannot recover bitmask without salt
Update delegation tests to pass blindingSalt input; verify scope-narrowing constraint still holds with salt present
Verify createAgentCredential returns unique blindingSalt per call; verify proveHandshake uses it correctly in input map
Add entry under 'Credential Privacy': scopeCommitment brute-force was a pre-salt vulnerability; cross-reference fix

steps: Audit current Poseidon usage in AgentPolicy.circom and Delegation.circom to confirm exact signal names and template inputs
Replace Poseidon2 component with Poseidon3 in AgentPolicy.circom: add `signal input blindingSalt`, wire as third Poseidon input
Apply identical Poseidon3 change in Delegation.circom; verify the parent→child scope narrowing constraint still uses the updated commitment signal
Recompile both circuits: `npm run compile:circuits` — confirm R1CS constraint counts increase by the Poseidon3 delta (~50 constraints per circuit)
Re-run phase-2 trusted setup for AgentPolicy and Delegation against pot16.ptau to produce new .zkey artifacts
Export updated vkey.json files and regenerate AgentPolicyVerifier.sol and DelegationVerifier.sol via snarkjs export solidityverifier
Extend AgentCredential TypeScript type with `blindingSalt: bigint`; update createAgentCredential to sample cryptographically random 32-byte salt via crypto.getRandomValues
Update proveHandshake input signal map to include blindingSalt from the stored credential
Mirror blindingSalt in Python SDK AgentCredential dataclass; update subprocess bridge JSON serialisation
Update fast circuit tests (witness-only) to supply blindingSalt; add hiding-property test asserting two salted commitments differ for same bitmask
Update slow circuit tests (full Groth16+PLONK proofs) to pass blindingSalt and confirm verifyProof succeeds
Update Hardhat integration tests: ensure verifyProof on-chain passes with regenerated verifier contracts
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md and circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md to document the hiding guarantee
Run full test suite: `npm test` — all fast + contract tests must pass; tag slow tests for CI gate
DCO-signed commit: `git commit -s -m 'fix: add blindingSalt to scopeCommitment (Poseidon3) in AgentPolicy and Delegation'`

winner.title: Bind sessionNonce to chainId to prevent cross-chain replay
winner.description: The current sessionNonce is chain-agnostic: a valid handshake proof generated for Base can be replayed on Arbitrum or Polygon if the same IdentityRegistry is deployed there with the same roots. Fix: change nonceBinding in HumanUniqueness and nullifierHash in AgentPolicy to include block.chainid as a Poseidon input (nonceBinding = Poseidon3(nullifierHash, sessionNonce, chainId)). Update IdentityRegistry.verifyHandshake to pass block.chainid as the expected chainId public signal. This is a prerequisite for any multi-chain deployment.

outline.title: Bind sessionNonce to chainId to prevent cross-chain replay

artifacts.description: Add chainId as a public input; change nonceBinding to Poseidon3(nullifierHash, sessionNonce, chainId) instead of Poseidon2
Add chainId as a public input; include chainId in the nullifierHash commitment: Poseidon3(secret, nonce, chainId)
Pass block.chainid as the expected chainId public signal to both verifyProof calls inside verifyHandshake; revert if signal mismatch
Regenerate Solidity verifier from updated HumanUniqueness.zkey after circuit recompile and new trusted setup
Regenerate Solidity verifier (both Groth16 and PLONK variants) from updated AgentPolicy.zkey
Add chainId parameter to proveHandshake and verifyHandshake; pass chainId as public input when generating witness; include in proof inputs object
Extend HandshakeOptions and ProofInputs types to include chainId: bigint
Propagate chainId through the subprocess bridge call; update Python type stubs for HandshakeOptions
Document chainId binding in Section on nonce construction; add cross-chain replay as a named threat in Security Considerations
Add invariant: nonceBinding is chain-specific — two proofs for distinct chainIds sharing the same sessionNonce must not be interchangeable
Add fast witness tests: (a) valid proof with correct chainId passes, (b) proof generated for chainId=1 fails witness check when chainId=137 is substituted
Mirror cross-chain replay rejection tests for AgentPolicy circuit
Integration test: mock proof with mismatched chainId signal causes verifyHandshake to revert; correct chainId passes
Unit test proveHandshake with explicit chainId; assert proof.publicSignals includes chainId at expected index
Update proveHandshake usage example to pass chainId; note that proofs are chain-specific and cannot be reused across deployments

winner.title: Write machine-checkable circuit invariant properties document
winner.description: Formalize the 12 core safety properties across all 4 circuits (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation, ModelInstanceBinding) as machine-checkable assertions: (1) no field overflow on uint64 inputs, (2) nullifier uniqueness per identity per scope, (3) scope monotonicity across delegation chain, (4) expiry monotonic narrowing, (5) Merkle root determinism, (6) nonce binding freshness, (7) EdDSA signature completeness, (8) cumulative bit invariant, (9) provider key binding (MIB), (10) delegation subset correctness, (11) credential commitment preimage binding, (12) no witness malleability on public outputs. Deliver as a structured markdown spec (circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md update) with each property stated in first-order logic and a companion test that checks the property via witness generation.

outline.title: Write machine-checkable circuit invariant properties document

artifacts.description: Updated spec with all 12 safety properties stated in first-order logic, covering HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation, and ModelInstanceBinding circuits. Each property includes a formal statement, circuit location reference, and violation condition.

Mocha test suite exercising all 12 properties via witness generation (no full proof required). Each test constructs a valid witness, then mutates exactly the relevant input to trigger a constraint violation and asserts that witness generation fails.
Shared test harness: wasm witness generator loader, valid-input fixture factory per circuit, and a mustFail(circuit, inputs) helper that asserts circom_tester throws on constraint check.

steps: Audit existing circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md for any pre-existing property stubs and collect all constraint names from HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, Delegation.circom, and ModelInstanceBinding.circom.

For each of the 12 properties, draft a first-order logic statement referencing circuit signal names (e.g., 'forall x: uint64, x < 2^64 => field(x) = x') and identify the specific constraint or template that enforces it.
Expand circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md: add a top-level table mapping each property to its circuit(s), FOL statement, enforcement mechanism (constraint name or template line), and companion test ID.

Write property-harness.js: loadWasm(circuitName) using circom_tester, buildValidInputs(circuitName) returning a known-good input object per circuit, and mustFail(circuitName, inputs) asserting witness generation throws.

Implement formal-properties.test.js with one describe block per property (12 total). Each block: (a) generates valid witness with buildValidInputs to confirm baseline passes, (b) applies a targeted mutation (e.g., set uint64 input to 2^64 for overflow check, replay nullifier for uniqueness check) and calls mustFail.

Cover property 3 (scope monotonicity) and property 4 (expiry narrowing) with multi-step delegation chain inputs in Delegation circuit tests, verifying that parent_scope ⊇ child_scope and parent_expiry ≥ child_expiry are both enforced.
Cover property 8 (cumulative bit invariant) by testing that setting a higher-tier bit without its implied lower bits fails the AgentPolicy circuit witness.

Cover property 12 (no witness malleability) by verifying that two distinct private inputs producing identical public outputs are rejected — use duplicate nullifier inputs across different scope signals.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm all 12 property tests pass and no pre-existing tests regress.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md header with a 'Test Coverage' section listing each property ID and its companion test file + test name for traceability.
dependencies: circom_tester npm package (already in devDependencies)
HumanUniqueness.circom compiled wasm in circuits/build/
AgentPolicy.circom compiled wasm in circuits/build/
Delegation.circom compiled wasm in circuits/build/
ModelInstanceBinding.circom compiled wasm in circuits/build/ (must exist or tests are skipped with a pending marker)
circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md (existing file to update, not replace)

winner.title: Specify DID resolution algorithm with on-chain state queries
winner.description: The existing did-method-bolyra.md defines the DID syntax but lacks a concrete resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7.1. Specify the read() and resolve() operations: how a resolver maps did:bolyra:<commitment> to a DID Document by querying IdentityRegistry.sol for enrollment status, revocation, and current Merkle root. Define the DID Document structure including verificationMethod (EdDSA on BabyJubJub), service endpoints for proof exchange, and deactivated metadata for revoked identities. Deliverable: updated spec/did-method-bolyra.md with resolution pseudocode, error codes, and 5+ test vectors in spec/test-vectors/.

outline.title: Specify DID resolution algorithm with on-chain state queries

artifacts.description: Updated DID method spec: W3C DID Core §7.1 compliant read() and resolve() operations with pseudocode, error codes (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, methodNotSupported, internalError), DID Document structure including verificationMethod (EdDSA/BabyJubJub), serviceEndpoints for proof exchange, and deactivated metadata for revoked identities

Standalone resolution algorithm document: step-by-step resolver pipeline from DID string parsing through on-chain IdentityRegistry.sol queries to DID Document construction, including caching hints and deterministic ordering rules
5+ test vectors covering: valid human DID resolution, valid agent DID resolution, revoked identity (deactivated=true), non-existent commitment (notFound), and malformed DID string (invalidDid)
Test vector schema documentation: field definitions, how to run conformance checks against the vectors, and expected resolver output format

Minimal Solidity interface exposing the read surface needed by resolvers: getEnrollmentStatus(commitment), getMerkleRoot(), isRevoked(nullifierHash), getAgentCredential(agentId) — documents the exact query pattern the resolution algorithm depends on

TypeScript implementation of the resolution algorithm: DIDResolver class implementing resolve(did, options) → DIDResolutionResult, wrapping ethers.js calls to IdentityRegistry and constructing the DID Document per spec
Unit tests for DIDResolver: mocked IdentityRegistry responses covering all test-vector scenarios, error path coverage, and DID Document structural validation
Operator guide: how to instantiate a Bolyra DID resolver, configure RPC endpoint for IdentityRegistry, and integrate with the W3C did-resolver npm package

steps: Audit existing spec/did-method-bolyra.md to catalog what DID syntax and partial resolution hints already exist; note gaps against W3C DID Core §7.1 checklist (read, resolve, error codes, DID Document fields)

Define the canonical DID Document structure: id field (did:bolyra:<commitment>), verificationMethod array (type EdDSAVerificationKey2022, publicKeyMultibase encoding of BabyJubJub point), authentication and assertionMethod referencing the verificationMethod, service array with type BolyraProofExchange pointing to proof-exchange endpoint

Define deactivated metadata handling: when IdentityRegistry.isRevoked(nullifierHash) returns true, resolver MUST set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true and return an empty DID Document body per W3C spec
Write pseudocode for parse(did) step: validate did:bolyra: prefix, extract <commitment> hex string, return error code invalidDid if malformed

Write pseudocode for read(commitment, options) step: call IdentityRegistry.getEnrollmentStatus(commitment) on the configured chain; if not enrolled return notFound; if revoked return deactivated; else return enrollment record with current Merkle root and BabyJubJub public key

Write pseudocode for resolve(did, options) step: call parse(), then read(), then construct DIDResolutionResult with didDocument, didDocumentMetadata (created, updated, versionId = Merkle root block number), and didResolutionMetadata (contentType application/did+ld+json, error if any)

Define all error codes as an enum with W3C-mandated string values: invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, methodNotSupported, internalError; document under which on-chain or parse conditions each is returned

Author 5 test vectors in spec/test-vectors/did-resolution-vectors.json: (1) enrolled human identity → full DID Document, (2) enrolled agent credential → DID Document with agent-specific service endpoint, (3) revoked nullifier → deactivated=true empty document, (4) commitment not in registry → notFound error, (5) malformed DID string missing prefix → invalidDid error
Draft IIdentityRegistry.sol interface exposing exactly the view functions the resolution algorithm queries, with NatSpec documenting the return types and revert conditions

Implement DIDResolver TypeScript class in sdk/src/resolver.ts: constructor takes ethers.Provider + registry address, resolve() method maps DID string to DIDResolutionResult, uses ethers multicall for batching getEnrollmentStatus + isRevoked in a single RPC round-trip
Write unit tests in sdk/test/resolver.test.ts using sinon stubs for the IdentityRegistry contract; assert exact DID Document field values against each test vector from the JSON file
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md in-place: add §7 Resolution section with the pseudocode, error code table, DID Document example, and a pointer to spec/test-vectors/
Write docs/did-resolution-quickstart.md: three-step operator guide (install @bolyra/sdk, configure resolver with RPC URL + registry address, call resolver.resolve(did))
Run tsc --noEmit on sdk/ to confirm TypeScript types are coherent; run npm test in sdk/ to confirm all resolver unit tests pass

Perform a final gap check: verify the spec language satisfies W3C DID Core conformance criteria §7.1 (all MUST/SHOULD terms accounted for), cross-reference against spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md for consistency
dependencies: contracts/IdentityRegistry.sol — production contract ABI needed to finalize IIdentityRegistry.sol interface and confirm view function signatures
spec/did-method-bolyra.md — existing syntax definition is the base document being extended
W3C DID Core §7.1 and §7.3 (read + resolve algorithm requirements) — normative reference
BabyJubJub public key serialization format — must confirm multibase encoding scheme (base58btc vs base64url) used in verificationMethod to align with circuits/AgentPolicy key output
@bolyra/sdk v0.2.0 public API — resolver module must not break existing createAgentCredential / proveHandshake exports
ethers.js v6 — required for on-chain IdentityRegistry queries in the TypeScript resolver
W3C did-resolver npm package interface — DIDResolutionResult type must match its expected shape for drop-in compatibility
